title: Add podman machine events
body:
Note: Automated tests WIP issues regarding holding socket open under ginkgo framework

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
If developer provides a unix domain socket whose name matches machine_events.*\.sock in  XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman
or by setting the environment variable PODMAN_MACHINE_EVENTS_SOCK, then podman machine will publish events to
that socket using the Event type from the events package rendered in JSON.
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jwhonce*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [jwhonce,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@baude Is @benoitf  's issue related to the socket length issue? Does util.GetRuntimeDir() take care for that for me?

> but when building from this branch I've the following error on all `podman machine` commands
> 
> ```
> ./bin/darwin/podman machine
> Error: lstat /var/folders/tg/_5rxbhmj4xncz4szvpgswrmc0000gn/T/podman-run--1/podman: no such file or directory
> ```

Hello,

If I understand well, either:

1. I need to add an environment variable before using podman machine commands `PODMAN_MACHINE_EVENTS_SOCK`
2. or I need to add a socket file to the runtime directory. 

Adding extra env is not possible as user may enter the commands he like on any terminal so we can't be sure that we can track events.

Option 2 looks promising but I don't see how we get the runtime directory easily

It's computed there
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/vendor/github.com/containers/common/pkg/util/util_supported.go#L30-L86

But there is no exposure of that directory using any CLI command so I don't see how easily (not error prone) I could add a machine_events.*\.sock in that directory ?

AFAIK this runtime directory should be exposed else I don't see how I could use it

I've opened https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14113

@Luap99: new pull request created: #14112

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14085#issuecomment-1117329629):

>/cherry-pick v4.1
